title: Gold prices rise on Middle East de-escalation signals, weaker dollar

summary: The oil futures market appears not to have caught up with the massive supply disruption from the Middle East yet—a supply shock that is now in its third month and lasting more than anyone expected in early March. title: API Confirms Very Large Crude and Oil Product Draws
summary: The American Petroleum Institute (API) estimated that crude oil inventories in the United States fell by 8.1 million barrels in the week ending May 1. title: Norway Just Switched on Another Gas Lifeline for Europe
summary: With little fanfare but clear strategic impact, Equinor has brought the long-forgotten Eirin gas field into production, sending fresh gas volumes into Europe’s system at a moment when security of supply still outweighs nearly every other energy priority. title: Gold and oil have been two of the hottest trades of the past one year. One of them has to break
summary: What's good for energy stocks may be bad for precious metals if the rally in crude means a rise in Treasury yields, which usually means lower gold prices.

title: U.S.-Canada Oil Pipeline Nears Go-Ahead as Shippers Lock In Volumes
summary: A long-delayed push to move more Canadian crude into the United States is back on the table, and this time it has barrels behind it. summary: The global economy will soon be running on fumes
